signals.title: College Students Are Rapidly Losing the Ability to Read
signals.summary: University instructor reports none of his students could complete a 20-page article. 12th grade NAEP reading scores at lowest since 1992. Nearly a third of 12th graders scored below 'basic' level.
signals.relevance: Directly affects every college student's academic experience; links to phone/social media usage debate
signals.reliability: Backed by NAEP 2024 assessment data — official federal statistics

signals.title: Are Many College Students Losing the Ability to Read?
signals.summary: Community discussion on declining college reading skills, referencing instructor anecdotes and NAEP data.
signals.relevance: Shows community buzz around reading decline topic

signals.title: The decline in reading comprehension
signals.summary: Tufts student newspaper covers reading comprehension decline among peers, citing smartphone reading producing lower comprehension scores vs paper.
signals.relevance: Campus-level student perspective on reading decline
signals.reliability: Student newspaper — reflects genuine student concern

signals.title: Canvas hack: What we know about apparent cyberattack that impacted thousands of schools
signals.summary: ShinyHunters hacking group stole 3.65TB of data from 275 million Canvas users across 8,809 institutions. Login page replaced with ransomware message during finals week. Rumored $10M ransom paid.
signals.relevance: Largest educational data breach in history — every student using Canvas was affected
signals.reliability: CNN reporting with confirmed details from Instructure

signals.title: Cybercrime group crashes Penn's Canvas system, demands ransom to prevent data release
signals.summary: Penn student newspaper covers how Canvas breach disrupted finals at their campus specifically.
signals.reliability: Campus newspaper with direct student quotes

signals.title: A major student loan overhaul takes effect July 1. Here's what borrowers need to know.
signals.summary: Major federal student loan changes July 1: new RAP plan (1-10% of income, $10 min payment), SAVE plan ends, Grad PLUS eliminated, new borrowing limits for grad students, 1% auto-pay interest rate reduction.
signals.relevance: Directly affects every student borrower — changes take effect in 9 days
signals.reliability: CBS News summarizing confirmed Department of Education final rules

signals.title: Guide: Student loan options change July 1. What you need to know
signals.summary: Comprehensive NPR guide to July 1 loan changes. RAP not indexed for inflation — modest income gains could trigger big payment increases. Only IDR plan for new borrowers after July 1.
signals.relevance: Explains trap: RAP not inflation-indexed, so payments can jump unexpectedly
signals.reliability: NPR — high reliability, sourced from DoE rules

signals.title: FAS Could Lay Off 25 Percent of Staff in Summer Administrative Restructuring
signals.summary: Harvard FAS faces $365M structural deficit. Could lay off up to 25% of staff this summer. Already laid off 55 alumni affairs staff, 35 SEAS staff, 38 IT workers. 3,600+ signed petition to halt layoffs.
signals.relevance: If Harvard ($57B endowment) is cutting this deeply, smaller schools are in worse shape
signals.reliability: Harvard Crimson — primary campus source with direct access

signals.title: Harvard has a $57 billion endowment. It's still eyeing staff cuts
signals.summary: Boston Globe covers Harvard layoffs paradox: $57B endowment yet massive staff cuts. Endowment tax hike and deferred maintenance driving deficit.
signals.relevance: Highlights shocking contrast — richest university still cutting
signals.reliability: Major regional newspaper with deep Harvard coverage

signals.title: Some GWU students to pay over $98,000 for 2026-27 year, estimate shows
signals.summary: GWU total cost of attendance approaching $98K/year for 2026-27. Tuition alone $72K (3% increase). With room, board, fees: ~$95K+.
signals.relevance: Shock-value number — almost $100K/year at one school

signals.title: Which Colleges Are Closing in 2026? A Running Tracker of 8 Shutdowns and 6 Mergers
signals.summary: 8 colleges closing in 2026 including Anna Maria College, Hampshire College, Siena Heights. 6 mergers including NJCU+Kean, Rosemont+Villanova. Small tuition-dependent liberal arts colleges in Northeast/Midwest at highest risk.
signals.relevance: Students at small colleges wonder if their school is next

signals.title: The 14 worst-paying college majors, 5 years after graduation
signals.summary: Theology, performing arts, social services, education all under $45K median. Foreign language majors lowest at ~$40K. Chemical engineering tops at $135K. Lifetime earnings gap can exceed $3.4M.
signals.relevance: Every student choosing or reconsidering a major cares about this

signals.title: To avoid accusations of AI cheating, college students are turning to AI
signals.summary: Students using AI 'humanizer' tools ($50/subscription) to beat AI detectors. 92% of students use AI for studying, 18% submit unedited AI work. Turnitin identified 150 humanizer tools.
signals.relevance: Every student navigating AI policies is affected. Ironic twist: using AI to hide AI use.

signals.title: College Dorms Are Terrible—and More Expensive Than Ever
signals.summary: Dorm costs rose 100%+ at public 4-year schools in 30 years. Room and board ($12,770) now exceeds tuition ($11,260) at public universities. UCF approved 21% single-occupancy housing increase.
signals.relevance: Housing surpassing tuition as biggest college expense is a shocking reversal
signals.reliability: The Nation with data from Education Data Initiative

signals.title: Summer 2026 Internships Still Open: What's Left in June + Fall 2026 Options
signals.summary: 90% of summer 2026 internships filled. FAANG+ paying $50-70/hr ($24K-33K for 12 weeks). 70%+ of interns at major firms get full-time return offers. NVIDIA $62/hr, Amazon Robotics $53/hr still have rolling openings.
signals.relevance: Students without internships feel urgency; pay gap between tech and other fields is stark
signals.reliability: Extern platform aggregating real job postings
